items.title: Miri’s main water artery springs leak, water supply disruption to affect over 70,000 consumers
items.summary: MIRI, June 10 — Sarawak Water Sdn Bhd (Sarawak Water) has confirmed that the 1,100mm main pipeline at Mile 7 Miri-Bintulu Road is the source of a major leak here. The list of areas in Miri, Sarawak affected by the water supply interruption. For more information, contact Sarawak Water on 085-426626.

items.title: Private road, public risk: Tourism minister urges Genting to step up safety measures
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 10 — More speed-reduction measures, including speed bumps, should be installed along roads in Genting Highlands to reduce accident risks and improve road safety, Tourism, Arts and Culture Minister Datuk Seri Tiong King Sing said today. “There is an urgent need to review and expedite the implementation of more technology-based monitoring measures to enhance road safety management. Tiong said his ministry would hold further discussions with Genting Highlands management to obtain a clearer picture of the resort’s current road safety management practices.

items.title: 22 traders, including 13 foreigners, nabbed in Pasar Pudu joint raid
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Twenty-two unlicensed traders, including 13 foreigners, faced enforcement action during a joint raid at Pasar Pudu, Jalan Yew, for operating illegally and obstructing public spaces. The raid, carried out by Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) and the Immigration Department at 6am yesterday, targeted unlicensed traders and foreigners operating in the busy market area. DBKL said the operation focused on people selling fresh produce, vegetables, fruits and other grocery items without valid licences, as well as foreigners suspected of breaching the conditions of their work permits.

items.title: Johor PAS commissioner says ties with Bersatu still friendly despite split
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 10 — Johor PAS will maintain friendly ties with Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia (Bersatu) at the state level despite having to officially sever ties, said Johor PAS Commissioner Datuk Mahfodz Mohamed. “The Johor Bersatu leadership had in fact contacted me regarding the decision (on Monday night). “Despite that, I assured him Johor PAS will continue our friendship with the state Bersatu,” he added Mahfodz, who is Johor PN deputy chairman, was responding to the recent announcement that PAS had cut ties with Bersatu, effectively ending its six-year political cooperation.

items.title: Malaysia to promise Japan maximum possible LNG and naphtha supplies
items.summary: TOKYO: Malaysia's prime minister will agree to provide Japan with the largest possible supplies of liquefied natural gas (LNG) and naphtha as Tokyo seeks to diversify its sources of energy and petrochemicals, the Nikkei newspaper reported on Wednesday. Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi and Malaysian Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, currently visiting Tokyo, are expected to formalise this pledge in a joint statement at a summit later on Wednesday, the report added. Japan faces a potential LNG supply crunch due to tensions linked to the Iran conflict, as air-conditioning demand in the country rises heading into summer.

items.title: Selangor to make universal design a development requirement under new PWD blueprint
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, June 10 — The Selangor govt today launched the Selangor Persons with Disabilities Policy and Action Plan 2026-2030 to strengthen inclusion and support the holistic development of the state’s approximately 150,000 registered persons with disabilities (PWDs). State Women Development and Social Welfare Committee chairman Anfaal Saari said the comprehensive policy marked a significant shift from a passive welfare-based approach towards one centred on rights, active participation and empowerment. On accessibility, Anfaal acknowledged that making older developments fully PWD-friendly could be challenging, but said improvements could begin with new developments.

items.title: #NSTViral: Runaway truck wheel narrowly misses vehicles on highway
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A motorist's dashcam captured the heart-stopping moment a runaway wheel narrowly missed vehicles on a highway. The 30-second video, which was later shared on Facebook by user Naz Kdas, shows both a heavy truck and a van travelling along the highway. Another user, Aidil Mt Said said: "What kind of engines are they using?

items.title: Johor PAS to contest state polls under loose pact with like-minded parties
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 10 — Johor PAS will contest the upcoming state election under an electoral pact with an alliance of Malay-Muslim political parties, after recently ending its political cooperation with Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia (Bersatu). Johor PAS Commissioner Datuk Mahfodz Mohamed said the pact was aimed at maximising the parties’ combined strengths. “Johor PAS has also not decided on the number of seats to contest for the upcoming Johor election.

items.title: Capital A's BigPay dispute escalates as Singapore court moves to seize shares
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Singapore Supreme Court has issued a notice to seize shares held by Capital A Bhd's unit Move Digital Sdn Bhd in BigPay Pte Ltd and Teleport Everywhere Pte Ltd. The company said the notice relates to arbitration proceedings brought by Christopher Davison and Navin Rajagopalan against Move Digital, AirAsia Bhd and BigPay at the Singapore International Arbitration Centre (SIAC) over disputes arising from shareholdings in BigPay. Move Digital intends to challenge the seizure.

items.title: Pentagon chief to visit Guantanamo as US ramps up Cuba pressure
items.summary: WASHINGTON: Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth will travel on Wednesday to the US military base in Guantanamo Bay, Cuba, to "engage with troops," the Pentagon said in a brief statement. In addition to Guantanamo, the Pentagon said Hegseth will also travel to Tampa, Florida, where US Central Command (Centcom) is headquartered. Late last month, the top US general overseeing operations in Latin America visited Guantanamo, where he met with Cuban military leaders.

items.title: Ringgit opens higher against major currencies, easier versus US dollar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit opened higher against major currencies but eased against the US dollar today, as market sentiment remained cautious ahead of the release of the United States Consumer Price Index (CPI) data tonight. Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said the foreign exchange market is expected to remain guarded ahead of the highly anticipated US CPI report. The ringgit traded higher against a basket of major currencies.

items.title: US House passes US$70bil bill to fund ICE, Border Patrol
items.summary: WASHINGTON: A longstanding political stalemate in the US Congress over money for immigration enforcement ended on Tuesday, when the Republican-controlled House of Representatives passed a US$70 billion bill to fund US Immigration and Customs Enforcement and Border Patrol through the remainder of President Donald Trump's administration. The 214-212 vote, which was roughly along party lines, sends the partisan legislation to the White House for Trump's signature. Democrats vehemently opposed funding for immigration enforcement after the fatal shootings of two Americans by immigration agents in January.

items.title: Rosmah to learn fate in RM68.3mil missing jewellery suit today
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Datin Seri Rosmah Mansor will know today whether she is liable for the loss of 43 pieces of jewellery worth millions of ringgit following a police raid on Pavilion Residences in 2018. The High Court is scheduled to deliver its decision in Lebanese luxury jeweller Global Royalty Trading SAL Ltd's US$14.57 million (RM68.3 million) suit against the wife of former prime minister Datuk Seri Najib Razak over the missing jewellery. The case centres on 44 pieces of jewellery that Global Royalty Trading claimed were supplied to Rosmah on a consignment basis but were never returned.

items.title: US trade judge urges Trump administration to speed up tariff refunds
items.summary: NEW YORK: A US trade judge on Tuesday urged Trump administration officials to speed up refunds of more than US$10 billion in revenue from tariffs that were collected and later deemed illegal by the Supreme Court, but stopped short of issuing a new order compelling them to do so. Eaton called the inequity an "unintended consequence" of how the government set up its system to return the US$166 billion in illegal tariffs and said he did not believe the administration was seeking to favour larger importers. But he said the Trump administration's decision to appeal his March 4 order requiring the government to refund all tariffs was delaying the payments.

items.title: Malaysia strengthens ties with global employers body to boost labour market
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia will continue strengthening its strategic cooperation with the International Organisation of Employers (IOE) to build a more inclusive, sustainable and competitive labour market ecosystem, says Human Resources Minister Datuk Seri R. Ramanan. He said the collaboration was vital in balancing economic growth, worker protection and employers' interests amid increasingly complex global labour market challenges. Ramanan said the IOE welcomed Malaysia's efforts to strengthen labour standards in line with global developments and international best practices.

items.title: Oil prices fall to seven-week low as Iran and Israel halt attacks
items.summary: Trump said Iran shot down a US helicopter in the Strait of Hormuz and he threatened that Washington would respond. Brent futures fell US$2.80, or three per cent, to settle at US$91.45 a barrel. US West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude slid US$3.10, or 3.4 per cent, to settle at US$88.20.

items.title: S&P 500, Nasdaq fall as tech selling resumes, Trump vows to respond to downed US helicopter
items.summary: NEW YORK: The S&P 500 and Nasdaq indexes fell on Tuesday as a rebound in technology shares faded and as President Donald Trump said the US must react to Iran's shooting down of a US helicopter. The S&P 500 tech index fell more than four per cent before paring losses. The tech index ended down 1.8 per cent and the semiconductor index was 1.9 per cent lower on the day.

items.title: Malaysia eyes central role in Asia-Pacific MRO growth as aviation demand rise [WATCH]
items.summary: This expanded facility is a direct contribution to Malaysia's aviation ambition, Loke said in his speech at the Collins Aerospace MRO expansion launch at Subang Aerotech Park on Tuesday. "Collins Aerospace has been part of the region's aerospace story for over 50 years. Collins Aerospace currently employs about 150 workers at the Subang facility.

items.title: Protesters torch buildings and vehicles, block roads over Belfast stabbing
items.summary: BELFAST: Anti-immigration protesters torched buildings and vehicles in Belfast on Tuesday evening and blocked roads, a day after a stabbing allegedly by a Sudanese refugee, captured in a graphic video that shocked the country. Hundreds of protesters, many masked, gathered at several locations across Belfast, AFP journalists saw. Suddenly the fire started going...we had smoke inside the building...fire people came in and they said 'go down'," he said in broken English.

items.title: 16 dead in a week sparks alarm over Malaysia's road safety crisis [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A string of fatal road accidents claiming at least 16 lives in just over a week has prompted renewed calls for Malaysia to tackle deeper road safety issues beyond individual accidents. Road safety and criminology experts said while investigations into each crash must proceed independently, the recent spate of fatalities highlights long-standing concerns involving speeding, risky driving behaviour, inadequate hazard perception and a road system that remains vulnerable to human error. "Whether we ride motorcycles or drive cars, we all share responsibility for road safety.

items.title: Qantas, low-cost arm Jetstar to launch services from new Western Sydney Airport
items.summary: BENGALURU: Qantas Airways said on Wednesday the Australian carrier and its low-cost arm Jetstar will launch commercial passenger services from the Western Sydney International Airport when it opens on Oct 25 later this year. When the new airport launches, Jetstar plans to operate up to 14 weekly flights to Melbourne, as well as four weekly flights to the Gold Coast and three to Brisbane. Qantas services will commence on March 28 next year, with four weekly flights each to Melbourne and Brisbane.

items.title: Throw repeat drugged drivers behind bars before more lives are lost: Experts [WATCH]
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Authorities should throw the book at repeat traffic offenders who drive while under the influence, including imposing longer jail sentences, as they pose a serious threat to public safety. Senior criminologist Datuk Dr P. Sundramoorthy said these drivers should not be treated as ordinary offenders. Driving offences involving aggravating factors should carry the possibility of substantial jail time, said Sundramoorthy.

items.title: Keeping children safe, even in court: Who are the victim liaison officers helping child victims and witnesses through legal proceedings?
items.summary: 1 of 2025 on the Operations of the Mobile Court for Children, a VLO is a Judiciary officer or staff member appointed by the Chief Registrar of the Federal Court to manage matters involving the use of the Children’s Mobile Court in criminal cases involving child victims or witnesses. Informally, VLOs serve as trusted figures whom children can turn to for guidance and reassurance during court procedures and testimony recording sessions. The small but crucial workspace where trained VLOs support children throughout the court process with care and compassion.

items.title: When words fail: How anatomical dolls help child abuse victims find their voice in court
items.summary: Enter the anatomical dolls — seemingly ordinary playthings with facial features (including a retractable tongue), limbs, clothing and realistic body proportions, including private parts — used by the Malaysian Judiciary in child witness proceedings. Though unable to speak themselves, these specially tailored anatomical dolls play a crucial role in giving child witnesses a voice when trauma, shyness or limited vocabulary makes verbal testimony difficult. The anatomical dolls feature facial characteristics, including a retractable tongue, as well as limbs, clothing and anatomically accurate body proportions, including genitalia.

items.title: US launches new strikes on Iran after helicopter downed
items.summary: WASHINGTON/BEIRUT/DUBAI: The United States on Tuesday launched strikes against Iran after President Donald Trump said Teheran had shot down a US Apache helicopter in the Strait of Hormuz, deepening doubts about prospects for peace between the two countries. "They shot down a helicopter, and we are responding as we speak," Trump told ABC News. Trump earlier said the two US pilots involved in the incident were uninjured.

items.title: Spain says end of joint fighter jet project 'worrying for Europe'
items.summary: MADRID: A decision by France and Germany to abandon a flagship joint fighter jet programme is "very worrying for Europe", Spain's Defence Minister Margarita Robles said Tuesday. "I think this is bad news, very worrying for Europe and for Europe's strategic autonomy," she said, adding: "the interests of industry have been placed ahead of the interests of Europe's security and defence." Berlin and Paris agreed on Monday to ditch the Future Combat Air System (FCAS) programme that was seen as a key test of European efforts to work together in the face of a hostile Russia.

items.title: Nick Reiner seeks trust fund money for parent murder defence
items.summary: LOS ANGELES: Nick Reiner, the son of legendary US movie director Rob Reiner, is demanding access to a US$1.5 million trust fund set up by the parents he is charged with murdering, court documents show. Nick Reiner is one of three children they had together. Rob Reiner also adopted a child during his first marriage.

items.title: Iran and Lebanon: Intertwined fronts in the Middle East war
items.summary: WASHINGTON: President Donald Trump's administration has sought – with very limited success – to separate Iran and Lebanon peace talks, while Teheran insists the two are linked, complicating efforts to resolve the intertwined conflicts. The Iran war began with a joint US-Israeli attack – though the interests of those two countries' leaders have since diverged – while Lebanese fighter group Hizbollah struck Israel shortly thereafter, sparking a devastating Israeli military response. The US president is striving to negotiate with Iran, prevent a wider regional war, stabilise energy markets and contain the crisis in the Strait of Hormuz – all while allowing Israel to pursue its military campaign in Lebanon, Toossi said.

items.title: China's Xi says he reached important consensus with Kim in North Korea visit
items.summary: SEOUL: Chinese President Xi Jinping said he and North Korean leader Kim Jong Un reached an "important consensus" and agreed to safeguard regional and global peace, in a message of thanks following his visit to Pyongyang, North Korean state media KCNA said on Wednesday. In the message carried in full by KCNA, Xi said the two sides had "exchanged in-depth views on issues of common concern and reached a series of important joint consensus", reflecting their determination to further deepen ties. Xi said the visit had established a deeper, more comprehensive understanding yielding a clearer path for development of ties, China's official Xinhua news agency said.

items.title: US expects to finish wall along Mexican border by late 2027
items.summary: WASHINGTON: The United States expects to complete President Donald Trump's long-promised southern border wall by late 2027, Customs and Border Protection (CBP) Commissioner Rodney Scott said Tuesday. "The primary border wall will be done by the end of 2027," he said. "There's a couple of gaps," Scott said.

items.title: Japan city relieved as bear caught after roaming for days
items.summary: A black bear that roamed the streets of a Japanese city for four days, forcing mass school closures, was caught yesterday, capping a search involving dozens of hunters and police. Yesterday, dozens of police officers, hunters and city officials surrounded a private home where the bear was spotted, an AFP photographer witnessed. AFP images showed the sedated bear loaded onto a truck.

items.title: KLIA expands Asia-Europe links
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia Airports Holdings Bhd (MAHB) is strengthening Kuala Lumpur International Airport's (KLIA) position as a regional aviation hub. The move comes amid shifting travel patterns, airline capacity adjustments and a more challenging global operating environment, according to MAHB shareholder Khazanah Nasional Bhd. MAHB's privatisation in early 2025, he said, marked a new chapter for Malaysia's airport sector.

items.title: Group proposes tax on sweetened condensed milk
items.summary: Putrajaya should introduce an excise tax on sweetened condensed milk and creamer as part of the government's "War Against Sugar" campaign under the 2027 Budget. Azrul Mohd Khalib, chief executive officer of health think-tank Galen Centre for Health and Social Policy, said yesterday the sugar-sweetened beverage tax was insufficient to tackle the growing non-communicable disease (NCD) crisis in Malaysia. "The Galen Centre urges the finance and health ministries to consider an excise tax on sweetened condensed milk and creamer in the 2027 Budget.

items.title: BTS fans lose RM96,000 to scammers
items.summary: Scammers are exploiting the frenzy surrounding K-pop group BTS' Kuala Lumpur concert by offering non-existent tickets, fake insider access and bogus priority passes. Bukit Aman Commercial Crime Investigation Department (CCID) director Datuk Rusdi Mohd Isa said police had recorded 41 cases linked to BTS concert ticket scams nationwide, with losses totalling RM95,974.81. He said CCID checks found syndicates and scammers actively using social media to advertise their ploy, particularly for highly sought-after categories, such as VIP, soundcheck and priority access passes.

items.title: Mexican president calls pre-World Cup protests 'provocation'
items.summary: MEXICO CITY: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum on Tuesday called protests by teachers ahead of this week's launch of the 2026 World Cup a "provocation." The left-leaning leader called the decision by the teachers' union to camp out near Mexico City's central fan zone "unexplainable" and a "provocation – as if to say, 'Look at how bad the situation is in Mexico.'" Sheinbaum said Thursday's opening game between Mexico and South Africa "is guaranteed" despite planned protests.

items.title: Pope Leo XIV met Bad Bunny in Madrid on Monday: Vatican
items.summary: BARCELONA: Pope Leo XIV held a surprise meeting with Puerto Rican music star Bad Bunny at Real Madrid's Bernabeu stadium during his state visit to Spain, the Vatican said on Tuesday. He (Bad Bunny) was with his family and some other people", and Leo "greeted them briefly before leaving the stadium", Vatican spokesman Matteo Bruni told reporters of the encounter on Monday. "I think many will see Bad Bunny.

items.title: Thailand officially frees ex-PM Thaksin after royal pardon
items.summary: BANGKOK: Thailand's former prime minister Thaksin Shinawatra is officially a free man, less than a week after receiving a royal pardon, the Department of Corrections said on Tuesday. The Department of Corrections said the former premier was required to wear an electronic monitoring device as a condition of his release. The corrections department said on Tuesday Thaksin's electronic monitoring device had been removed.

items.title: Speaker urges stronger laws, political will to safeguard children’s rights
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 9 — Dewan Rakyat Speaker Tan Sri Johari Abdul said lawmakers have a responsibility to ensure legislation reflects the best interests of children and remains responsive to emerging challenges. Johari said strengthening children’s rights and welfare requires not only effective legislation but also sustained commitment and cooperation across all levels of government and society. “Protecting children is a shared responsibility.

items.title: Palace declares Melaka hotel ceremony to proclaim Negeri Sembilan Ruler void, strips Nadzaruddin of hereditary title
items.summary: Comptroller of the Palace Datuk Azizi Mohamad Ali said the title of Yang di-Pertuan Besar that Tunku Datuk Seri Nadzaruddin Tuanku Ja’afar sought to assume, carries no legal effect. “However, by attempting to accept that invalid title, Tunku Datuk Seri Nadzaruddin ibni Almarhum Tuanku Ja’afar has, by his own action, relinquished the hereditary title of Tunku Panglima Besar of Negeri Sembilan that he previously held,” he said in a statement this evening. Azizi said the hereditary title of Tunku Panglima Besar is now vacant as a result.

items.title: Tractor slams into Sik house, driver pronounced dead at scene
items.summary: SIK, June 9 — A man was killed after the agricultural tractor he was driving veered off the road before crashing into a tree and a house pillar in an incident at Kampung Sungai Senam near here this morning. Sik police chief Supt Shamzury Saman said the 42-year-old victim was believed to be travelling from his company to his workplace in Kampung Sungai Senam when the incident occurred at about 8.30 am. “Upon reaching the scene, the victim was believed to have suddenly lost control of the vehicle, causing it to veer onto the right road shoulder and crash into a tree and a nearby house pillar.

items.title: Film a cop, hurl abuse, get arrested: Sentul police nab Proton Prevé owner over traffic summons
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 9 — Police arrested a 25-year-old local man early today for verbally abusing and recording a police officer who was issuing a traffic summons yesterday. Sentul Police Chief ACP Basri Sagoni said the suspect was apprehended at a business premises in Jalan Dato Senu at 2.36 am. Basri said police also seized a mobile phone and a SIM card believed to have been used in the incident.

items.title: Orang Asli reps seek action after viral video claiming Tok Batin who helped search for Jaslinda confined hiker and planned to marry her
items.summary: TAPAH, June 9 — About 50 Tok Batin and Orang Asli representatives from three districts lodged police reports today over what they described as slanderous allegations circulating on social media against the Tok Batin of Kampung Orang Asli Lubuk Gaharu, Mohd Asmmadi Abdullah. Lata Kinjang Tok Batin Rani Bah Ulin said the reports were lodged after a social media user allegedly uploaded videos containing baseless accusations that tarnished Mohd Asmmadi’s reputation and the Orang Asli leadership institution. “The Tok Batin is a respected community leader and the pillar of Orang Asli leadership.

items.title: Zahid: BN to go solo in Negeri Sembilan after Pakatan lays claim to all 36 seats
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 9 — Barisan Nasional (BN) will contest the upcoming Negeri Sembilan state election solo, said its chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi. The Umno president said BN had no choice but to go solo after Pakatan Harapan (PH) announced its intention to contest all 36 state seats. Ahmad Zahid described the synergy within the existing team as very strong, with all parties working closely together.

items.title: Bersatu excos can stay in Kedah govt if they back PAS-led administration, says MB
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, June 9 — Bersatu state executive councillors can remain in the Kedah government despite PAS ending political cooperation with the party. Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Muhammad Sanusi Md Nor said the only criterion was that they continue to support the PAS-led state government, news portal Malaysiakini r eported today. Asked whether retaining Bersatu excos contradicted the party's stand, Sanusi said it did not.

items.title: Malaysia’s fuel subsidy jumped from RM700m in January to RM5b in March before peaking in April, says finance minister II
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 9 — Malaysia’s monthly fuel subsidy bill surged to as high as RM7.5 billion in April amid rising global oil prices triggered by tensions in West Asia, Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan said. He said the subsidy bill rose sharply from about RM700 million a month in January and February to RM5 billion in March before peaking in April, although it has since moderated as oil prices eased. Amir Hamzah said the government’s immediate priority was ensuring uninterrupted fuel supply to support economic stability.

items.title: Anwar: Malaysia ready for next industrial leap with Japan in AI, digital and energy sectors
items.summary: TOKYO, June 9 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has urged Japanese companies to continue expanding investments in Malaysia, particularly in high-technology sectors such as semiconductors, artificial intelligence, quantum computing and digital industries. Anwar said Malaysia is prioritising emerging technologies such as AI, digital industries and quantum computing while continuing to support traditional sectors including oil and gas. The Prime Minister, who is also Finance Minister, expressed appreciation for the Japanese business community’s long-standing role in Malaysia’s economic development.

items.title: AG: No legal breach in Ahmad Terrirudin’s Judicial Appointments Commission role
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 9 — The Attorney General has maintained that the Malaysian Bar has failed to establish any breach of the Judicial Appointments Commission (JAC) Act in relation to the appointment of Federal Court Judge Tan Sri Ahmad Terrirudin Mohd Salleh as a member of the commission. He said Section 5(1)(e) of the JAC Act sets out only two conditions for JAC membership — that the individual must be a Federal Court judge, and that the appointment must be made by the Prime Minister. He was submitting arguments in opposition to the Malaysian Bar’s application for leave for judicial review challenging Ahmad Terrirudin’s appointment to the JAC.

items.title: Digital Ministry unveils new Hansard system for searchable parliamentary records
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 9 (Bernama) -- Parliament has introduced a new Hansard management system aimed at improving the efficiency of parliamentary administration through a more integrated approach to recording and accessing official debate records. Digital Minister Gobind Singh Deo said the strategic initiative, implemented through GovTech Malaysia, was designed to provide faster and more systematic access to key parliamentary documents while reducing reliance on physical copies. Meanwhile, the Digital Ministry, in a statement, said that the new system transforms the traditional PDF-based Hansard format into a smarter digital platform.

items.title: Safe drivers may get up to 65pc insurance savings under Cermat Madani, says transport minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 9 — Motorists with good driving records could see total motor insurance savings of up to 65 per cent under the Cermat Madani initiative, which introduces an additional risk-based rebate on top of existing no-claim discounts. Transport Minister Anthony Loke said the framework is designed to better reflect driving behaviour in insurance pricing, rewarding low-risk motorists through data-based assessments. “Today, we launch Cermat Madani, an initiative aimed at encouraging a culture of safer, more responsible and disciplined driving through the use of relevant information and data in assessing motor insurance risk,” he said at the launch.

items.title: Immigration Dept detains 30,801 foreigners in nationwide enforcement up to May 31
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 9 — The Immigration Department detained a total of 30,801 foreign nationals for various offences as of May 31 this year. Immigration director-general Zakaria Shaaban said 2,324 of those detained had misused their immigration passes, including by taking part in activities that were not in line with the purpose of their entry into Malaysia. Zakaria said the steps taken include establishing an Enforcement Inspectorate, conducting targeted operations in identified hotspots, taking firm action against employers who violate immigration regulations, and strengthening integrated operations with relevant enforcement agencies.

items.title: Human skull believed to be adult male found in fishing net off Sedili waters
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 9 — A human skull believed to belong to an adult male was found in the waters off Sedili near Kota Tinggi after it became entangled in a local fisherman’s net on June 6. Kota Tinggi police chief Supt Yusof Othman said the skull was discovered by a 44-year-old boat skipper at about 3 am before being handed over to the police. He said the skull was subsequently sent to the Forensic Unit of Kota Tinggi Hospital for further examination.

items.title: Hiker Jaslinda looks forward to recovery after nearly two weeks lost in Gunung Batu Putih
items.summary: TAPAH, June 9 — After nearly two weeks lost in the Gunung Batu Putih area and three days in hospital, hiker Jaslinda Saludin says she is looking forward to returning home to rest, while promising to address speculation surrounding her disappearance at a later date. Seen leaving the hospital at about 1.50 pm with a walking stick and her left foot bandaged, Jaslinda said her immediate priority was to recover. Jaslinda also said she would give a statement at an appropriate time to address speculation surrounding her disappearance.

items.title: Mohamad Sabu: Malaysia needs National Food Security Act to tackle supply risks
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 9 — The Executive Committee Meeting on the National Food Security Policy No. 1 of 2026, convened today, highlighted the need to introduce the National Food Security Act to strengthen Malaysia’s readiness in addressing food supply issues. The meeting, among others, aimed to chart a more inclusive direction for national food security and recommend a focused strategy to address food security issues, thereby ensuring Malaysia remains resilient amid increasingly dynamic global challenges.

items.title: Perhilitan confirms report of Malayan tiger sighting in Gua Musang
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 9 — The Department of Wildlife and National Parks (Perhilitan) has confirmed receiving a report regarding the sighting of a Malayan tiger near the Sungai Relai Bridge in Gua Musang, Kelantan, yesterday, following the circulation of a video of the animal on social media. According to a Bernama report, its director-general Datuk Abdul Kadir Abu Hashim said investigations carried out by the Gua Musang district office found that the tiger was believed to have merely crossed the area before returning to its natural habitat. He added that Perhilitan would continue monitoring the area to ensure public safety and support ongoing conservation efforts for the Malayan tiger.

items.title: Media bodies back national pledge on responsible suicide reporting in Malaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUPUR, June 9 — More than 200 editors, journalists and media professionals have endorsed a national pledge on safe, ethical and responsible reporting of suicide-related content, in a collective industry move aimed at strengthening newsroom practices on sensitive public health coverage. According to organisers, the pledge was signed by representatives from the Malaysian Media Council, the National Union of Journalists and Gabungan Kelab Media Malaysia during a programme titled “Reporting Suicide: A Newsroom Guide to Safer Coverage”, jointly organised by the Communications and Multimedia Content Forum of Malaysia (CMCF) and the National Centre of Excellence for Mental Health (NCEMH), Ministry of Health Malaysia, at the Putrajaya International Convention Centre. The signing was witnessed by Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil and Health Minister Datuk Seri Dzulkefly Ahmad, underscoring a shared commitment between media practitioners and policymakers to promote safer reporting practices.

items.title: Metal‑cutting caused former Odeon Cinema blaze in Penang that killed Myanmar worker, probe finds
items.summary: Penang Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) director Mohamad Shoki Hamzah said forensic investigations found the fire was accidentally triggered during renovation works at the premises. “CCTV footage showed two of the three workers carrying out metal cutting work using an oxy-cutter set before hot molten metal residue fell onto combustible materials below and ignited a fire. Mohamad Shoki said inspections by the department’s K9 Detection Unit found no traces of fire accelerants at the scene.

items.title: Sarawak Water: Tests tonight to trace major leak and determine whether 20,000 or 70,000 Miri consumers face water outage
items.summary: Sarawak Water acting chief executive officer Malseni Jamal said the assessment would involve temporarily shutting off a 750mm valve to establish whether the leak originates from the 750mm pipeline or the larger 1.1 metre pipeline. Malseni said if the leak is traced to the 750mm pipeline, the areas affected would be Taman Jelita, Taman Tunku, Jalan Engkabang, Sungai Rait, Eastwood, Hilltop, and Riam. Other affected areas would include Kampung Beraya, Kuala Sibuti, Bukit Song, Sungai Karab, Bukit Peninjau, Beluru town area, and Sungai Seruas.

items.title: Penang CM says rise in state reserves predates quit rent increase
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, June 9 — Penang Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow today dismissed claims that the state’s cash reserves had risen to more than RM600 million because of the recent quit rent hike, saying the increase was driven by a surplus recorded in 2025. He said the balance in the state’s Consolidated Revenue Account had already increased before the revised quit rent rates came into effect on January 1 this year. Chow said the state recorded a surplus in 2025, with cash reserves exceeding RM500 million as of December 31, 2025.

items.title: Budget 2027 to stay development‑driven amid crisis‑year pressures, says Akmal Nasrullah
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 9 — The Ministry of Economy will continue to focus on development expenditure in Budget 2027, which is expected to be tabled this October, said Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir. He said all ministries have submitted their development proposals, and evaluations are being conducted at the ministry level through a bidding mechanism. Akmal Nasrullah said a detailed review of those development plans will begin next month.

items.title: Chow: Penang seeking DoE input before deciding on Jelutong landfill rehabilitation project
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, June 9 — The Penang government has met the Department of Environment (DoE) to better understand the environmental impact assessment (EIA) considerations for the proposed Jelutong landfill rehabilitation and Karpal Singh Drive reclamation project, Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said today. Chow said the state government’s decision-making process must be guided by verified information and proper procedures. He said the state government must follow the proper process as any decision would have implications, particularly financial ones.
